mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows, or forward returns show no statistical significance during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.5 percent, labor market slack reduces wage inflation pressures and signals economic weakness, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in 65 percent of observations when UNRATE exceeds 5.5 percent, invalidating the threshold effect.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand growth, which drives corporate earnings expectations higher and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform individual series, or forward returns are negative in 70 percent of co-expansion periods.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, a decline in the industrial production index signals capacity constraints and impending growth deceleration, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: The signal loses predictive power outside late-cycle regimes, or forward returns are positive in 60 percent of late-cycle INDPRO declines.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the consumer price index above 0.3 percent forces markets to price in aggressive central bank tightening, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive when CPIAUCSL MoM exceeds 0.3 percent, or the threshold shows no statistical edge over a linear model.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects robust economic activity and rising corporate revenues, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.01 over rolling 3-year windows, or payroll growth fails to correlate with quarterly equity returns.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising GDP growth and falling retail sales indicates inventory buildup and weakening final demand, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: The interaction term shows no incremental predictive power, or forward returns are positive in 65 percent of divergence episodes.

mechanism_narrative: In post-shock recovery regimes, a rebound in industrial production signals restored manufacturing capacity and supply chain normalization, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The signal performs identically across all regimes, or forward returns are negative in 60 percent of post-shock INDPRO rebounds.
